content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Looking at atom N:0 (blue sphere on the right side), I can clearly see it is connected to:

1. S:1 (yellow sphere, sulfur) - directly bonded
2. H:31 (white sphere, hydrogen) - directly bonded
3. H:32 (white sphere, hydrogen) - directly bonded

The bonds are visible as cylinders connecting these atoms to N:0. There are exactly three covalent bonds from the nitrogen atom.

From the file name "geomconf_5286.xyz", I can confirm that the geomconf_index for this molecule is 5286.

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 0 (N) are:
- Atom index 1, element S (sulfur)
- Atom index 31, element H (hydrogen)
- Atom index 32, element H (hydrogen)

And the geomconf_index for this molecule is 5286.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 0, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
